title: HTTP Caching Headers
topic: HTTP caching headers — Cache-Control, ETag, and conditional requests
summary: A short, example-first guide to Cache-Control freshness, ETag/conditional-GET revalidation, and choosing a caching strategy per endpoint.

images.prompt: A clean, modern flat-vector editorial illustration: on the left a simple browser-window icon, on the right a simple server-rack icon, connected by a road. Midway along the road, a translucent amber shelf holds a glowing duplicate document — the cached copy. A small paper airplane travels from the browser toward the server on one pass; on a second, lighter pass only a tiny empty arrow returns, suggesting a fast confirmation instead of a full document making the trip again. Cool signal-blue and warm amber accent palette on a near-white background, generous whitespace, minimal geometric style, no text or letters baked into the image. Target size ~1200×800px, 3:2 landscape; compose with the subject centred and safe margins — it is letterboxed into a 3:2 box, so keep nothing important near the edges.
images.alt: A browser and a server exchanging a stamped response ticket, with a cache shelf holding a stored copy between them
images.caption: A stored copy, a stamp, and a much shorter round trip.
